calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Sara Eisenberg, Esq. of San Francisco Deputy City Attorney, 415.554.4633, appearing for the Petitioner City and County of San Francisco;
Atty. Suzanne Solomon, Esq. of Liebert Cassidy Whitmore, 415.512.3000, appearing for the Respondent San Francisco Board of Education, et al.

Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction.

Matter called. Counsel appear to address the issues set forth in the Court's tentative ruling and in the Court's email sent to counsel this morning prior to this afternoon's hearing:

Issues raised in the Court's tentative ruling:

1. Whether the action is moot in light of developments since it was filed on February 3, 2021, including but not limited to (i) the Memoranda of Understanding entered into between the District and unions representing District employees, and (ii) the District's updated in-person learning plan under which it intends to offer in-person instruction starting on April 12, 2021.

2. Whether the statutory directive that the District offer in-person instruction "to the greatest extent possible" (Educ. Code Sec. 43504(b)) imposes a clear, present and ministerial duty that leaves no room for an exercise of discretion or judgment by the District.

3. Precisely what injunctive relief the City is seeking, and whether such relief is susceptible to judicial enforcement. In particular, what does the City believe would comprise "a plan to reopen for all grades in a reasonable time and manner" (Reply at 14)? What standards or criteria does the City contend the Court should apply in devising such proposed relief?

Issues raised in the Court's email to Counsel:

1. AB 86 (Stats. 2021, ch. 10 (eff. Mar. 5, 2021)), particularly the provisions of Educ. Code Sections 43521 and 43520.

2. The San Diego Superior Court's March 15, 2021 order in A.A. v. Newsom, No. 37-2021-00007536-CU-WM-NC.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions on the issues raised by the Court, the matter is taken under submission.

The Court also stated that DISABILITY RIGHTS EDUCATION AND DEFENSE FUND, DISABILITY RIGHTS CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Leave To File Brief As Amici Curiae In Support Of Plaintiff And Petitioner City And County Of San Francisco is GRANTED.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Motion For Leave To File Brief As Amici Curiae In Support Of Plaintiff And Petitioner City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Mar-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
OTHERS DISABILITY RIGHTS EDUCATION AND DEFENSE FUND, DISABILITY RIGHTS CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Leave To File Brief As Amici Curiae In Support Of Plaintiff And Petitioner City And County Of San Francisco.
